rule: The library allows formatting collections of POCOs into structured console tables.
Table output supports multiple predefined visual formats and configurable column data alignment.
Table columns must be initialized before adding data rows, and every row must contain exactly the same number of items as the defined columns.
Cell content is formatted and aligned based on data type and configuration, including text wrapping at delimiters, right-alignment of numeric columns, and Base64 conversion of binary data.
Table row counts are displayed at the bottom of the output only if the EnableCount configuration is enabled.
Tables can be configured to suppress the display of the total row count.
Column text alignment can be customized, allowing for configurations like right-alignment for numerical data.
Console tables support construction from dictionary data structures where keys represent row identifiers.
Table formatting must ensure visual alignment by accounting for Unicode character widths and supporting configurable column positioning, such as right-alignment for numeric data.
Tables must be capable of being generated from various data sources, including Lists, Dictionaries, DataTables, and Object Arrays.
Table output must support multiple formats (standard, Markdown, and Minimal) and can be redirected to any TextWriter, defaulting to the console standard output.
